Miami-Dade County FHA Loans

FHA loans across Miami-Dade County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, where FHA fits in a high-cost market, and how to get pre-approved.

Miami-Dade County Home Loans

FHA loans in Miami-Dade County, Florida

Miami-Dade is Florida's largest and most diverse county — a high-cost, condo-heavy market where home prices run well above the statewide norm. That makes the FHA program both essential and nuanced here: it opens the door for first-time and working-family buyers, but you have to know where it fits. The good news is Miami-Dade's high-cost FHA limit gives you more borrowing room than most of Florida, and affordable pockets like Hialeah and Homestead stretch it further.

Miami-Dade County FHA limit (1-unit)Above floor

What the FHA limit means in Miami-Dade County

Miami-Dade County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is set above the $541,287 statewide floor — giving you more borrowing room than most of Florida. Because published figures vary for high-cost counties, we confirm Miami-Dade County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup before you shop. That extra room matters in pricey Miami-Dade, though luxury homes and many waterfront properties still exceed it — FHA buyers focus on condos and mid-market homes.

Condos, affordability, and FHA in Miami-Dade

Two themes define FHA here. First, condos: much of Miami-Dade's inventory is condos, and FHA requires the building to be FHA-approved or the unit to qualify for Single-Unit Approval (see our condo guide). Second, affordability: areas like Hialeah and Homestead offer the county's most attainable prices, where FHA's 3.5% down goes furthest. Many buyers also use FHA 2–4 unit loans to live in one unit and rent the others.

Cities We Serve

FHA loans in Miami-Dade County cities

We help FHA buyers throughout Miami-Dade County, including Miami, Hialeah, Homestead, Doral. Explore our local guides for a closer look at the market, loan limits, and down payment help in each:

MiamiHialeahHomesteadDoral

Down payment help in Miami-Dade County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Miami-Dade County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Miami-Dade County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Miami-Dade County for 2026?+
Miami-Dade is a designated high-cost county, so its 2026 FHA limit is above the $541,287 Florida floor. Published figures vary, so confirm the exact figure with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup.
Where does FHA work best in Miami-Dade?+
On condos (in FHA-approved buildings) and in more affordable areas like Hialeah and Homestead. Luxury and waterfront homes often exceed FHA limits.
Can I buy a Miami condo with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. We verify status before you offer.
Can I use FHA to buy a 2-4 unit property in Miami-Dade?+
Yes — FHA allows owner-occupied 2-4 unit homes with 3.5% down, and rental income from the other units can sometimes help you qualify.
Do you serve all of Miami-Dade County?+
Yes — Miami, Hialeah, Homestead, Doral, Miami Gardens, and every community in the county.
